---
title: "Chad Hazlett"
pagetitle: "Chad Hazlett"
toc: false
about:
  template: trestles
  image: assets/headshot.jpg
  image-shape: round
  image-width: 14em
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:chazlett@stat.ucla.edu
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/chadhazlett
    - icon: mortarboard-fill
      text: Google Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=pbD0dGwAAAAJ
    - icon: journal-bookmark
      text: Practical Causal Inference
      href: https://practicallycausal.com
---

I am a faculty member at UCLA, jointly appointed in the Departments of
[Political Science](https://polisci.ucla.edu/person/chad-hazlett/) and
[Statistics & Data Science](https://statistics.ucla.edu/).

My research develops methods for causal inference — tools that let us learn
honestly from data when randomized experiments are impossible or impractical.
I work on three connected methodological threads:

- **Identification strategies** that clarify what assumptions are doing the
  work, and what we can and can't conclude when they fail.
- **Sensitivity analyses** that let researchers communicate honestly about
  how much their conclusions depend on those assumptions.
- **Estimation and modeling tools** — kernel-based regression, balancing
  methods, and related techniques — that relax functional-form assumptions
  without giving up interpretability.

I apply these tools to questions in political violence and mass atrocity
prevention, environmental and health policy, and medicine. I co-direct the
[Practical Causal Inference lab](https://practicallycausal.com) with
[Onyebuchi Arah](https://ph.ucla.edu/about/faculty-staff-directory/onyebuchi-arah),
and the [Early Warning Project](https://earlywarningproject.ushmm.org/)'s
statistical risk assessment with the
[US Holocaust Memorial Museum](https://www.ushmm.org/).

See the [Research](research.qmd), [Publications](publications.qmd),
[Teaching](teaching.qmd), and [Software](software.qmd) pages for more, or
download my [CV](cv.qmd).
